title: Potential bug in evaluating combinatorial wrapping to 0
body:
Hi, I think I may have encountered a bug in Verilator version 5.034. I'm using cocotb to invoke Verilator for simulating a FIFO.

I'm checking the FIFO is full by matching the write pointer address against the read pointer address as:
`assign FIFO_FULL  = (((wr_adr + 1)) == rd_adr);`
For this case FIFO full doesn't assert even when the wr_adr value reaches 511 (where wr_adr + 1 would hit 512 and wrap to equal 0). Instead to force verilator to reach the correct value I have to wrap the addition with a modulo operator (and add a lint off rule):
`assign FIFO_FULL  = (((wr_adr + 1)%512) == rd_adr); /* verilator lint_off WIDTHEXPAND */`

It also works on the synchronous logic for wr_adr if it's incremented past 511. So I suspect this is just a width check issue on the combinatorial side.

I've attached the full testbench for more detail.
[verilator_test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19929707/verilator_test.zip)
